title: The Last Mile — Workforce Reentry
description: Post-release career placement, mentorship, and continuing-education modules from TLM's Workforce Reentry program.
bestFor: TLM alumni and tech-curious returning citizens.

title: The Last Mile — Programs
description: In-prison coding, web development, audio/video production, and project management/sales/entrepreneurship modules.
bestFor: Anyone exploring tech-pathway training inside or after release.
location: 8+ states (CA, IN, OK, MI, ND, MT, MA, RI/PA expanding)

title: CSUF Project Rebound
description: Higher-ed support for formerly incarcerated students at Cal State Fullerton — scholarships, mentoring, tutoring, career placement, John Irwin Memorial House housing.
bestFor: Returning citizens pursuing a college degree in Southern California.

title: CSU Project Rebound (system-wide)
description: Network of CSU campuses supporting formerly incarcerated students. Find a campus near you.
bestFor: Returning citizens anywhere in California considering CSU.

title: CFPB — Your Money, Your Goals: Focus on Reentry
description: Federal consumer-finance toolkit specifically for working with returning citizens. Worksheets for goals, banking, debt, ID, background-screening rights.
bestFor: Anyone building a personal money plan after release.

title: FDIC Money Smart for Adults
description: Free 14-module financial-education curriculum with realistic scenarios. Available in multiple languages and accessible formats.
bestFor: Building day-to-day money skills (budgeting, banking, credit, debt).

title: National Reentry Resource Center
description: Federal hub for reentry information. Includes Clean Slate Clearinghouse (record-clearance policies by state) and the National Inventory of Collateral Consequences of Conviction.
bestFor: Understanding rights, record clearance, and collateral consequences.

title: DOL Reentry Employment Opportunities (REO)
description: U.S. Department of Labor program funding workforce services for justice-involved adults and youth.
bestFor: Job training, apprenticeships, and re-employment services.

title: 211 (United Way)
description: Free 24/7 information line for housing, food, healthcare, financial assistance, and crisis services. Dial 211 or use the website.
bestFor: Immediate help — food, shelter, utilities, mental-health crisis.

title: Findhelp
description: Searchable directory of 600,000+ free and reduced-cost programs. Search by ZIP code.
bestFor: Finding local food, housing, transportation, legal aid quickly.

title: CDC — Reentry Health
description: Federal guidance on health continuity at release: medications, chronic conditions, mental health, substance use.
bestFor: Planning healthcare in your first 30 days.

title: SAMHSA Helpline
description: Free, confidential 24/7 referral for mental-health and substance-use services. Call 1-800-662-HELP (4357).
bestFor: Mental-health or substance-use support.

title: 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline
description: Free, confidential 24/7 crisis support. Call or text 988.
bestFor: Mental-health crisis, suicidal thoughts, emotional distress.

title: Social Security — Get a New Card
description: How to replace a Social Security card after release. Often required before the first paycheck.
bestFor: Replacing a lost or unavailable SSN card.

title: ID.me — State ID & DMV Help
description: Many states issue an ID at release; check your state DMV's reentry program.
bestFor: State ID, driver's license reinstatement.

title: Lifeline — Free / Low-Cost Phone Service
description: Federal program providing discount phone or internet service to qualifying low-income households.
bestFor: Getting a reliable phone for jobs and case management.

title: Apprenticeship.gov
description: U.S. DOL apprenticeship search. Earn-while-you-learn programs in trades, tech, healthcare, and more.
bestFor: Trade skills with paid training and a credential.

title: CareerOneStop — Reentry Services
description: Department of Labor career resource: job search, training programs, American Job Centers near you.
bestFor: Resume help, local job centers, training funding.

title: AnnualCreditReport.com
description: Federally authorized site for free weekly credit reports from the three major bureaus. Check for identity theft.
bestFor: Reviewing what's on your credit before you apply for housing or jobs.

title: IdentityTheft.gov
description: FTC checklist if your identity was used while you were incarcerated.
bestFor: Reporting and recovering from identity theft.

title: HUD — Reentry Housing Resources
description: Federal housing programs including public housing, Section 8, and special programs for returning citizens.
bestFor: Long-term housing options after release.

title: USA.gov Benefits Finder
description: Find federal and state benefits you may qualify for: SNAP, Medicaid, TANF, unemployment.
bestFor: Stacking benefits while you re-enter the workforce.

title: John Irwin Memorial House (CSUF)
description: Reentry housing with wraparound support for formerly incarcerated CSUF students.
bestFor: Students enrolled at CSUF.

title: Root & Rebound (legal aid)
description: California-focused reentry legal advocacy: record clearing, housing, family law, employment rights.
bestFor: California residents needing legal info post-release.

title: The Last Mile — Official Site
description: Mission, programs, students, news, and the official press kit. Start here to learn about TLM directly.
bestFor: Anyone wanting first-source information about The Last Mile.

title: Stand Together — TLM Reentry Story
description: Stand Together's profile of The Last Mile and how its training prepares incarcerated individuals for reentry careers.
bestFor: Sharing TLM's story with employers, family, donors.

title: Wikipedia — The Last Mile
description: Encyclopedia article on The Last Mile program, founders, and history. A neutral overview to share with skeptics or curious supporters.
bestFor: A quick neutral overview of TLM's history.

title: Veterans Reentry Search Service (VRSS)
description: VA program connecting incarcerated veterans to VA benefits and reentry services.
bestFor: Veterans returning home.
